MARINER_REQUEST [mdm-a1b2c3d4]: Create a full linkedin account for Sarah Johnson.

Profile details (JSON):
{
  "name": "Sarah Johnson",
  "platform": "linkedin",
  "oneLineSummary": {
    "oneLineSummary": "Create LinkedIn for Sarah Johnson senior marketing executive",
    "firstName": "Sarah",
    "lastName": "Johnson",
    "headline": "Senior Marketing Executive B2B Growth Specialist",
    "email": "sarah.johnson.mktg@outlook.com",
    "password": "SecurePass2024"
  }
}

## HOW TO EXECUTE — follow these steps in order using the exec/bash tool:

CRITICAL: ALWAYS write dispatch tasks to a JSON file to avoid shell quoting issues.
Use this pattern for ALL dispatch calls:
  python3 -c "import json; json.dump(<TASK_DICT>, open('/tmp/task.json','w'))"
  node /data/director/dispatch.js /tmp/task.json --timeout 120

STEP 1 — The initial task file is pre-written. Start the browser:
  node /data/director/dispatch.js /tmp/mariner-task-init-mdm-a1b2c3d4.json --timeout 90

STEP 2 — Check the screenshot at /tmp/ss-mdm-a1b2c3d4-start.png to see what page loaded.
  Read it with: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const img=fs.readFileSync('/tmp/ss-mdm-a1b2c3d4-start.png'); console.log('size:'+img.length);"
  Or use the browser screenshot tool if available.

STEP 3 — If not logged in to linkedin: navigate to signup and fill in profile details.
  Write a task JSON file then run dispatch.js. Available step actions:
  - goto: {"action":"goto","url":"https://..."} — navigate
  - click: {"action":"click","selector":"<CSS selector>"} — click element
  - type: {"action":"type","text":"...","selector":"<optional CSS>"} — type text (optionally click first)
  - fill: {"action":"fill","selector":"<CSS>","text":"..."} — clear field and type
  - screenshot: {"action":"screenshot","path":"/tmp/ss.png"} — take screenshot
  - getPageText: {"action":"getPageText"} — get page text content
  - wait: {"action":"wait","ms":1000} — wait milliseconds
  - snapshot: {"action":"snapshot","ref":"snap1"} — get ARIA element list with refs
  - act: {"action":"act","ref":"e5","kind":"click"} — interact using snapshot ref

STEP 4 — Verify the account was created / profile is updated.

STEP 5 — When done (success or failure), POST to this webhook using exec/curl:
  curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"type":"account_created","execution_id":"test-exec-live-002","session_id":"mariner-mdm-a1b2c3d4-057844a7","account_id":"mdm-a1b2c3d4","client_name":"Sarah Johnson","platform":"linkedin","status":"complete"}' 'https://wlowwprkjdhvfecsxsvp.supabase.co/functions/v1/mariner-webhook'

  On failure:
  curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"type":"account_created","execution_id":"test-exec-live-002","session_id":"mariner-mdm-a1b2c3d4-057844a7","account_id":"mdm-a1b2c3d4","client_name":"Sarah Johnson","platform":"linkedin","status":"failed","error":"actual error"}' 'https://wlowwprkjdhvfecsxsvp.supabase.co/functions/v1/mariner-webhook'

IMPORTANT: Use exec to run bash/node commands. Do NOT use ACP or sessions_spawn.
Do NOT try to install anything. Do NOT use --reset-session or other openclaw flags.
